entities.name: theory
entities.description:
Represents a physics theory, concept, or model. Theories can be hierarchical
(parent-child relationships) and contain assumptions, postulates, claims, papers,
and associated people. Theories are organized by category (e.g., Classical Mechanics,
Quantum Physics, Relativity) and have a year of development.

fields.name: theory_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the theory (e.g., 'general-relativity', 'newtons-laws')

fields.name: name
fields.description: Display name of the theory (e.g., 'General Relativity', 'Newton's Laws of Motion')

fields.name: year
fields.description: Year when the theory was developed or published (four-digit calendar year; omit if unknown)

fields.name: summary
fields.description: Brief summary or description of the theory

fields.name: category
fields.description: Category or field of physics

fields.name: parent_theory_id
fields.description: Reference to parent theory for hierarchical organization

fields.name: assumptions
fields.description: List of assumptions underlying this theory (nested children)

fields.name: postulates
fields.description: List of fundamental postulates of this theory (nested children)

fields.name: claims
fields.description: List of claims or consequences made by this theory (nested children)

fields.name: predictions
fields.description: List of testable predictions made by this theory (nested children)

fields.name: anomalies
fields.description: Array of anomaly IDs that challenge or are explained by this theory

fields.name: papers
fields.description: Array of paper IDs referencing key papers related to this theory

fields.name: people
fields.description: Array of person IDs referencing people associated with this theory

fields.name: tags
fields.description: Array of tag IDs for categorizing and searching theories

fields.name: nobel_year
fields.description: Year when a Nobel Prize was awarded for this theory (if applicable; four-digit calendar year; omit if unknown)

fields.name: children
fields.description: List of child theories (nested sub-theories)

entities.name: assumption
entities.description:
Represents an assumption or premise that underlies a physics theory. Assumptions
are background conditions, implicit premises, or contextual constraints that are
taken as given. They may be simplifying conditions (e.g., "no friction", "isolated
system") or structural premises (e.g., "spacetime is a 4D manifold"). Unlike postulates,
assumptions are often implicit or contextual rather than explicitly stated as
foundational principles. They provide the framework within which the theory operates.

fields.name: assumption_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the assumption

fields.name: theory_id
fields.description: Reference to the parent theory

fields.name: statement
fields.description: The assumption statement or description (e.g., 'spacetime is a 4D manifold', 'no external forces act on the system')

fields.name: rationale
fields.description: Explanation of why this assumption is made, its justification, or the context in which it applies

entities.name: postulate
entities.brief: A fundamental postulate of a theory
entities.description:
Represents a fundamental postulate or axiom of a physics theory. Postulates are
explicitly stated foundational principles that are taken as true without proof and
from which other results are derived. They are the core building blocks of the theory,
often called "laws" or "principles" (e.g., "Equivalence Principle", "Einstein Field
Equations"). Unlike assumptions, postulates are explicitly stated as the fundamental
axioms of the theory and are typically the primary principles from which claims and
predictions follow.

fields.name: postulate_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the postulate

fields.name: theory_id
fields.description: Reference to the parent theory

fields.name: statement
fields.description: The explicitly stated postulate or principle (e.g., 'Equivalence Principle', 'Einstein Field Equations: G_μν = 8πG/c⁴ T_μν')

fields.name: formulation_year
fields.description: Year when this postulate was formulated (may differ from theory year, as some postulates may predate the full theory; four-digit calendar year; omit if unknown)

entities.name: claim
entities.brief: A claim or consequence of a theory
entities.description:
Represents a claim or consequence derived from a physics theory. Claims are
theoretical statements or consequences that follow from the theory's assumptions
and postulates. Unlike predictions, claims may be more general theoretical
consequences that are not necessarily testable in isolation.

fields.name: claim_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the claim

fields.name: theory_id
fields.description: Reference to the parent theory

fields.name: statement
fields.description: The claim or consequence statement

fields.name: status
fields.description: Status of the claim: theoretical (not yet tested), verified (confirmed by experiment), falsified (contradicted by evidence), pending_verification (under investigation)

fields.name: verification_year
fields.description: Year when the claim was verified or falsified (if applicable; four-digit calendar year; omit if unknown)

entities.name: prediction
entities.brief: A testable prediction made by a theory
entities.description:
Represents a specific, testable prediction made by a physics theory. Predictions
are concrete, falsifiable statements that can be experimentally verified or
falsified. They are distinct from claims in that they are explicitly testable
and have both a prediction year (when the prediction was made) and potentially
a verification year (when it was tested). Predictions are the bridge between
theory and experiment.

fields.name: prediction_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the prediction

fields.name: theory_id
fields.description: Reference to the parent theory

fields.name: statement
fields.description: The testable prediction statement (e.g., 'Moving clocks run slower than stationary clocks', 'Light rays are bent by gravitational fields')

fields.name: prediction_year
fields.description: Year when this prediction was made or published (four-digit calendar year)

fields.name: status
fields.description: Status of the prediction: pending (not yet tested), verified (confirmed by experiment), falsified (contradicted by evidence), partially_verified (some aspects confirmed)

fields.name: verification_year
fields.description: Year when the prediction was experimentally verified or falsified (if applicable; four-digit calendar year; omit if unknown)

fields.name: verification_method
fields.description: Description of how the prediction was verified or tested (e.g., 'Hafele-Keating experiment with atomic clocks', 'Gravitational lensing observation')

fields.name: people
fields.description: Array of person_ids who made this prediction or were involved in its verification

fields.name: papers
fields.description: Array of paper_ids related to this prediction (prediction papers, verification papers, etc.)

entities.name: paper
entities.description:
Represents a scientific paper, publication, or key reference related to physics theories.
Papers can be associated with multiple theories and people.

fields.name: paper_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the paper (e.g., 'einstein-1915-field-equations')

fields.name: title
fields.description: Title of the paper

fields.name: year
fields.description: Year of publication (four-digit calendar year; omit if unknown)

fields.name: link
fields.description: URL or link to the paper (e.g., Wikipedia article, DOI, etc.)

fields.name: authors
fields.description: Array of person IDs who authored this paper

entities.name: person
entities.description:
Represents a person associated with physics theories, papers, or discoveries.
People can be associated with multiple theories and papers.

fields.name: person_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the person (e.g., 'albert-einstein', 'isaac-newton')

fields.name: name
fields.description: Full name of the person

fields.name: birth_year
fields.description: Year of birth (four-digit calendar year; omit if unknown)

fields.name: death_year
fields.description: Year of death (if deceased; four-digit calendar year; omit if unknown)

fields.name: nobel_years
fields.description: Years when this person received Nobel Prizes (four-digit calendar years)

fields.name: sources
fields.description: Array of source descriptions tracking why this person was added to the database (e.g., 'referenced by paper feynman-1949-space-time', 'author of paper einstein-1905-relativity')

entities.name: tag
entities.description:
Represents a tag or keyword for categorizing and searching physics theories.
Tags help organize theories by themes, concepts, or properties (e.g., 'force', 'time', 'energy').

fields.name: tag_id
fields.description: Unique identifier for the tag (e.g., 'force', 'time', 'exergy')

fields.name: name
fields.description: Display name of the tag

fields.name: explanation
fields.description: Explanation of what this tag represents and how it relates to theories
